_meta.purpose: Culturally-diverse Texas-authentic name pool for question generation. Reflects Texas K-12 demographic mix (Hispanic ~40%, White ~28%, Black ~12%, Asian ~5%, Multi/Other ~15%). Anti-stereotype rule: any name from any pool can pair with any occupation, hobby, sport, science interest, or family structure.
_meta.provenance_marker: [CLAUDE-SYNTHESIZED] from Census name-frequency data, Texas Demographic Center reports, and common Texas given-name patterns. Names selected for: (a) STAAR-style readability at K-12 grade levels, (b) avoiding overly trendy spellings that age out fast, (c) representing actual ethnolinguistic communities present in Texas — Mexican/Mexican-American, Anglo, African-American, Vietnamese-American, South-Asian American, etc.
_meta.anti_stereotype_rule: When the generator picks a name + occupation/activity, randomize independently. NEVER use ethnic-sounding names as a proxy for ethnic-stereotyped activities (e.g., not 'Diego at the food truck', not 'Aanya in advanced math'). Mix it up: Diego runs the science club, Aanya plays football, Sofia builds robots, Carlos teaches dance.
_meta.sourcing_note: First-name pools weighted toward Texas-common given names per US SSA name-frequency data and Texas Department of State Health Services birth-record name frequencies. Last-name pools reflect Texas surname mix per Census surname tables. Specific name selection avoids overly-distinctive or attention-grabbing names that would distract from the math/reading content.

_usage_notes.for_generators: Pick a name pair (first + last) by sampling from a single demographic pool. So 'Sofia Martinez' (both hispanic) reads natural; 'Sofia Patel' (mixed) is OK in modern contexts but less common.
_usage_notes.frequency_target: Across a generated batch of N questions, the demographic distribution of names should approximate the target above (40/28/12/10/10). Don't sample 100% from one pool.
_usage_notes.avoid_repetition: Within a single API call's set of questions, don't reuse the same first name. The cold-start generator's name-injection (CLAUDE.md §30) shuffles 5 names per call from this pool — that pattern continues here.
_usage_notes.sensitive_combinations: A few names appear in multiple pools (e.g., 'Maria' is hispanic AND traditional Anglo Catholic; 'Sofia' is hispanic AND modern Anglo). That's fine — it reflects real Texas naming patterns. Just don't game it: the goal is realistic diversity, not maximizing pool overlaps.
_usage_notes.spelling_consistency: Use one spelling per name; don't randomize 'Sofia/Sophia/Sofía' — pick one (Sofia, no accent) and stay consistent.
